role_title: Strategic Initiatives & Corporate Affairs Lead, Office of the CEO

location.office_time_required: Denver, CO preferred; US Remote also available

level_signals: 6-10 years of experience in strategy, operations, corporate affairs, or related roles
Reports to VP, Office of the CEO & Corporate Affairs
Comp range $126K-$173K base consistent with Senior IC at a mid-size SaaS company
High level of discretion and professionalism expected

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6 -10 years of experience in strategy, operations, corporate affairs, or related roles within a high-growth SaaS or technology company.
Experience working on cross-functional projects and supporting initiatives that span multiple teams.
Strong communication skills, with experience developing content for senior leaders or executive audiences.
Understanding of B2B technology businesses and how companies position and operate in competitive markets.
Ability to work effectively with senior stakeholders and navigate ambiguity.
Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple priorities at once.
High level of discretion and professionalism in handling sensitive information.
Willingness to learn, take ownership, and operate in a fast-paced environment.
Willingness to travel up to 10% of the time.

scope.surface: Office of the CEO — strategy coordination, corporate affairs, executive effectiveness, cross-functional initiatives
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / integration and automation platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Executive team alignment and operating cadence quality
Clarity and consistency of Boomi's external narrative
On-time execution of cross-functional strategic initiatives
cross_functional_partners: VP Office of the CEO & Corporate Affairs
core_responsibilities: Drive day-to-day coordination across priorities, support executive workflows, and ensure key initiatives, meetings, and follow-ups are well-structured and moving forward.
Help bring Boomi's narrative and positioning to life across key audiences, ensuring clarity and consistency in how we communicate internally and externally.
Lead and support high-priority, cross-functional initiatives on behalf of the CEO and executive team, helping move work forward from concept through execution.
Support how Boomi shows up with customers, partners, and the broader market through content, coordination, and preparation for key moments.
Assist in preparing for critical moments including executive communications, customer and partner engagements, and leadership forums.
Help coordinate leadership cadences and forums to ensure alignment on priorities, decisions, and follow-through.
Operate with a high level of discretion and judgment, effectively representing the Office of the CEO across internal and external interactions.

green_flags: Role has clear scope tied to CEO office with defined responsibility areas
Denver preferred but US remote also eligible — good location flexibility
Encourages candidates with impostor syndrome to apply
High-visibility role reporting to VP with direct CEO-adjacent exposure
red_flags: Responsibilities described as 'flex based on business needs' — scope may be undefined or shifting
No equity or bonus mentioned in compensation section, which is unusual for a senior strategic role
Work authorization and visa sponsorship not addressed
Role title and archetype (Chief of Staff flavor) may not match internal leveling or title, leading to ambiguity about career progression

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + years + reporting level)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description as B2B SaaS integration platform)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
location.work_auth and location.visa_sponsorship (not mentioned in JD; marked unclear)
location.type set to 'hybrid' — JD says 'Denver, CO (preferred) or US Remote'; treated as hybrid with remote option since Denver preference implies in-office expectation is possible
ambiguities: Location: JD states 'Denver, CO (preferred) or US Remote' — unclear whether Denver-based candidates are expected in-office regularly or if 'preferred' is just geographic preference. Classified as hybrid given the preference language, but could also be remote-us.
No explicit mention of bonus, equity, or total comp beyond base — unclear if this is intentional omission or an all-base structure.
